messages.text: The mixing auger sensor is not reading. We are waiting for maintenance to arrive and check it
Sensor will not read on with the green light.  I unplugged it and plugged it back in hoping the reset would get it to work with no success.
Can you open and then close the motor plate?
We already did, but it didn’t work.
Thanks Preston it wasn’t all the way aligned.  It’s on now
The line started at 6:38 a.m.
We have 3 clogged nozzles.
The nozzles have already been unclogged.
Imperial belt, metal detector belt and C6 belt are running correctly
The first adjustment to the mixing water is made at 7:24 AM.

Weight check update 


The weight looks good; we are within the parameters required by the product specifications.

Tempering chart check update 


The temperature remains stable—slightly high, but within the permissible range required by the Centerline.

Packaging ZS check update 


The flow-wrap packaging is in good condition; it looks good and is very well sealed.
This is the ingredient test that we did on the line before we started.
We are stopping frequently Cavanna because the paper keeps splitting open.

Thank you for the update. 

Do you have maintenance there looking at it yet?
A second adjustment is made to the mixing water at 9:41 AM, bringing it to 83.6.
Yes, Nathan Ruiz has been checking Cavanna. Right now it is running much better.
Cavanna was running well but the paper began to open again, we changed the side of the roll and it improved, is adjusting the rollers
Chiller is working and machines have dropped to tempering temperatures

Justin Charity Curt Kathleen
Team,

Please be aware that both Sollich 2 and Sollich 3 will require sanitation verification activities prior to startup tomorrow morning. I will be the confirmed QA representative onsite performing the QA verification activities beginning at approximately 5:30 AM.

Sollich 2:
Previously identified sanitation verification and environmental monitoring requirements from the weekend communication still apply prior to startup. Please ensure all previously identified S2 swab locations are re-sanitized prior to QA verification and environmental swabbing activities.



Sollich 3:
Due to the drain work performed, Sollich 3 will require a targeted re-clean and heavy floor wash prior to QA release activities. At a minimum, sanitation must include:

Belts before and after the drain area
Framework surrounding the drain work
Enrober areas potentially impacted
Heavy wash and cleanup of the surrounding floor and splash zones

Following the initial cleaning, QA will perform ATP verification and environmental/micro swabbing as needed. After swab collection is completed, Sollich 3 will require a final re-sanitize prior to startup approval.

Please ensure both lines are cleaned, accessible, dry, and in a non-running state prior to QA verification activities.

Current target remains:

QA onsite: ~5:30 AM
Startup/product to back: ~7:00 AM pending acceptable sanitation and verification results

Please escalate immediately if cleanup timelines shift or if any concerns are identified during sanitation activities.
Good morning. The person assigned to deliver the warehouse materials has not arrived yet.
I am onsite at the warehouse desk. S2 Enrober
Matrix 6 is not opening the bags correctly. Maintenance is currently adjusting the suction cup arm.
The internet is too slow; it's not possible to connect to Safety Chain. The metal detector verification has been completed.
X-Ray form Done
X-Ray form Done

S2. Pouches hitting the floor instead of going into X-ray reject bin. 
- Operators do not understand once on floor these are scrap. They are picking up and putting into X-ray bin or trying to put through X-ray again. Only QA can put through X-ray again because it has to be a double pass. 
- Pouches that are on the floor still should be X-ray checked because they were rejected. 

Victor Romero Sr. Katya Farnsworth 

Need solution.
Can we sani wipe the pouches that fall on the floor?
Is there a written process available that we can train our operators to? Charity Young
Victor Romero Sr. Let's talk to the operators about not re-sending pouches through the Xray. Only QA can do that.
Trays have been placed in the X-ray machine so that the bag does not touch the floor and we can reuse it.
The operators and employees already know that they must not let through any bags rejected by the X-ray machine. Katya Farnsworth
Trays and bins were placed in the South matrices’ X-ray area to prevent fallen bags from touching the floor and being returned to the conveyor belt.

Hi Team,

The plan for today is to cook Classic Caramel 20001. In the Kitchen, we will test the re-work machine. On the Sollich line, we will check that everything is functioning properly. In the Twist packaging area, we will also test to make sure everything is working well.Curt Willis Justin Garner Jorge Carrillo Jorel Gourdin
Is the plan to run wax paper or cellophane today? Jorge Carrillo
I believe we wanted to flip between the both of those to see how well operators can do a changeover. Correct me if I am wrong. Jorge Carrillo Curt Willis

Team, 
 
Today we are changing over to Classic Caramel and the white wax twist wrap film. We ran out of one ingredient needed for Bequet caramel, so we are unable to produce more at this time. However, we can still change over to the Bequet cellophane film if needed.
 
One challenge is that last week, after we ran out of Bequet caramel, we continued testing the Bequet cellophane film using Classic caramel. Cornell mentioned that Classic caramel is softer than Bequet caramel, so the machine setting adjustments needed for Classic will be different than those needed for Bequet.
 
Request:
Curt Willis 

 Could Production please provide a point of contact to help align on labor support and materials needed for testing at the line this week?You had mentioned we needed sellable product. Do you know the target quantity needed and whether Quality has approved the line for sellable production yet?
Jazmin Vazquez, per our discussion, we need additional white wax film. Our current supply will run out in about two hours. Please let me know the ETA on the delivery.
We need sellable product?  Not until next week.
Last week you mentioned we needed some Classic Caramel, if this is no longger the case thats fine.  I'll just focus on testing.
Sorry we might make 6 cases of classic and Sea Salt if possible
Hi Team, Jesus (Lead) needs to come to Sollich 4. We already started, please. Nathan Topham
Marcos Jimenez can you please have Daniel shadow Jesus, so that he can learn how to manage guillotine?
Yes
Hello Jorge, more wax paper has been requested. Level 1, ETA is about 2-4 hours.

Curt Willis, Justin Garner, & Katya Farnsworth.   Could the POC for the S4 startup please come over to the line?  We need to align on a couple of things to help this commissioning week be as productive as possible.
The chocolate from the branch pump to the depositor is completely solid. We are having to a reheat.
Pipe is unclogged. We are back tempering.
We are depositing.
1 Shift
WDS Centerlines
First shift shadow board and centerlines
Viscosity test results: ok.  ✔️:
Line is down. Cross conveyor is not tracking. Nose piece was put on backwards.
Line back up running
Line is down. Bars are going sideways as they enter the FW causing jams. Maintenance is here.
Line is back up
Chocolate adjustment report Alan Todd Brandon Lance
Tightened drive side belt.
There is a caramel barrel and it's in the limit of Activity Water range. It was retested: the result was ok. In the range.:
Piece Weight Check, Packaging and dimensions:
Line is back up. Maintenance replaced speed belt #3
Line is down. On of the belts is rubbing on something causing FM. Maintenance is here investigating root cause.
Line down for CIL
Water test results: ✔️ pass
Line is back up
Line stopped. The mold temperature controller has stopped heating the molds.
Powering down mold warmer
It has corrected itself with the power down
Line running again
Amy Hadrich, Curt Willis
Nidia Lopez Corea
We are doing a cycle count.
Line was running as of 8:05 am
CL matrix 3,4,5 check weight  and pacraft matrix
Enrober tempering chart.
Enrober CL.
Bed and caramel CL.
Sanitizer was applied to the enrober drain area earlier today after the heavy wash.

It was noticed on these bolts they are housed into poistion with a tool rather then hand tight Justin.  Our current plan is to remove all guarding in order to have maintenance fix the bowed guarding that hitting on every lug.

At this time, maintenance found a small piece of metal on belt C26 in Matrix 5. This matrix is not running at the moment. The source is still unknown, and the corresponding investigation will be conducted.
Charity Young, Miriah Manzanares, Mary Montilla
It was noticed the z convayor was starting to snag on something as we started with product in the back.  Our team is going to take it apart to investigate.
Correction the line is still down not only for the z conveyor but also due to metal being found on MM5  Justin
I passed the metal through the Metal Detector and X-R on M3, M4, and M5, and it was not detected.
We cannot continue running until we identify where it came from, since it is not detectable.
The line is stopped now ,Charity, Justin and Nathan check in this moment

FM not detected by MD or X-ray. 

Plan- visual inspection with flashlights of all belts & matrix top/bottom to end of line. Starting with C19 through the X-ray. 

Justin Garner Miriah Manzanares let’s discuss Tunnel 2 - C2
All product scrapped & destroy bags on pallet too.
All product scrapped & destroy bags on pallet too.
Charity we have checked tunnel 2 and there was no FM in the filters

Good evening,

Packaging area looks good.
Filler area looks good.
We are in temper, just waiting for chocolate to be brought over from L1.
Chocolate has been staged! Alex Mendoza
Thank you

We are depositing

Centerlines

All the chocolate available on the line is in the melter. We have enough chocolate for about 2 more hours of run time. Alan Todd

Alex Mendoza, Jeffrey Barney

Please leave 10% in the buffer tank to avoid a reheat if we run out
Front door is unlocked.  I will deadbolt it and it will need to be unlocked tomorrow
Do we know who left this here and can wee get it moved please.
Thank you!
I did use it on Sunday for duct cleaning. I will move it

I have a feeling that this air gun was provided by a contractor but please remind your teams that we can only have OSHA approved air guns. This one has been confiscated. 
Curt, Justin, Dakota, Jody

William  Lopez Chris Givens

It sounds like there is work being done on the roof around this hole   Was anyone informed,
Area is being taken care of right now.
Roofers are patching the holes on roof
Curtain not sealed. Curt Willis Justin Garner
We’ll get this fixed.
Guys are getting barrels prep to start with the push. PTL is currently at 80%

Tuesdays clean.
The ones circled are priority.
Push on north side.
We need to disassemble north sweco, since as soon as we turned it on there’s a noice coming from the inside and it looks like there is a loose screw inside there vibrating.
Preston Tregaskis
Disassembled sweco and found this inside.
Cream tank is being re cleaned.
Mirella could you inspect the area and make sure there is not metal shavings.
TG#4 is ready, just waiting on QA to come and do the ATP. Isis Oses
Like we discussed, visually it looks good now, but we want to try letting it sit with the lids open to see how well that natural air circulation helps with the scent.
Cream tank is now re cleaned, we will leave it with the lid open and will monitor smell.
The area was inspected and no metal residues were found, also clean production to ensure that there is no foreign material
Thanks Mirella
Jenny Medina just tested chocolate from the push and she approved.
We also need to push licking roller and back off plow pipes. They still have oil.
Update, operator is installing depositor 1 right now. and we are preparing chocolate to push licking roller and back off plow pipes.
Need to re clean depositor #4.
Which shift cleaned Depositor #4 last week?
3rd shift.
